specific_mechanism: Necrophagous insects colonize a corpse in predictable stage-bound waves: Calliphoridae (blowflies) within hours, Sarcophagidae (flesh flies), Silphidae (carrion beetles) at active decay, Dermestidae (skin beetles) at advanced decay, and Tineidae (clothes moths) at dry remains. Each guild signals a specific decomposition stage; the assemblage acts as a temporal stratigraphy, and post-mortem interval (PMI) is reconstructed by reading which guilds are present and absent.
llm_application: Stage-bound 'guild assemblage' of probe signals for runtime hallucination detection: instead of one generic confidence probe, deploy a panel of stage-specific detectors keyed to distinct generation-pipeline failure modes — (a) entity-fabrication probes (early generation), (b) attribute-binding-error probes (mid-generation), (c) citation/date-hallucination probes (late generation), (d) snowball-error cascade probes (long-form). Read the present/absent pattern of detector firings to localize the post-error interval (PEI) — when along the generation trajectory the hallucination first emerged. Information-cascade analogy: the succession of detectors mirrors the succession of arthropod guilds.
novelty_claim: Hallucination-detection surveys (preprints 202510.0540, arXiv 2510.06265, ICLR 2026 papers) catalogue many detection methods but treat them as alternatives. Drowzee (2502.13416) uses temporal logic for fact-conflicting hallucinations. None proposes a forensic-stratigraphic ensemble that uses the pattern of which detectors fire (and which DO NOT) to localize the generation-step at which the hallucination crystallized. Open question: is 'stage-bound detector ensemble for PEI estimation' substantively new, or is it implicit in cascade verification literature?
